description:
Entry-point automation for new applicants. Fires on hr.applicant.on_create
and runs two checks in sequence:

1. Source detection — scans partner_name + email_from for keywords like
   "via Indeed" / "via LinkedIn" / "OnlineJobs.ph" and sets utm source
   accordingly. Cleans "via XXXX" suffix from the applicant name.

2. Resume gate — emails the "Request for Resume" template ONLY when:
     - The applicant has no attachments AND the job requires a resume,
     AND
     - The applicant came through one of the auto-trigger channels:
       Facebook, Jobstreet/SEEK, or the website careers form.
   Every other channel (Indeed, LinkedIn, OnlineJobs, HR manual
   creates, or any unknown channel) is excluded from the auto-trigger
   and HR follows up manually.

This server action NEVER writes stage_id. n8n owns the Stage 1 → Stage 2
transition.

Duplicate detection is intentionally NOT done here — HR handles
duplicates manually.

Ships the template, the server action, and the on_create automation.
The two custom fields the gate reads (hr.applicant.x_studio_resume_request_date
and hr.job.x_studio_resume_required) already exist on every target
env — on prod via Studio, on dev via changeset 002's initial push,
on staging via the prod-clone — so we do NOT re-declare them here.

Pure procedural script: the helper functions in the original block 01
script were inlined to remove safe_eval closure risks.
